sanskrit:
सञ्जय उवाच
एवमुक्त्वार्जुनः सङ्ख्ये रथोपस्थ उपाविशत् ।
विसृज्य सशरं चापं शोकसंविग्नमानसः ॥ ४६ ॥
transliteration:
sañjaya uvāca
evam uktvārjunaḥ saṅkhye rathopastha upāviśat |
visṛjya saśaraṃ cāpaṃ śokasaṃvignamānasaḥ || 46 ||
literal_meaning: Sañjaya said: Having thus spoken on the battlefield, Arjuna, his mind agitated by sorrow, cast aside bow and arrows and sat down on the chariot-seat.
contextual_meaning: A top warrior experiences a sudden freeze response: cognitive dissonance collapses into somatic shutdown—he disarms himself and withdraws, signaling acute stress-induced paralysis.
psychological_state: Acute stress reaction with dissociative withdrawal; sympathetic overload shifts to parasympathetic collapse.
trigger_scenario: A seasoned surgeon enters the OR, sees the patient is a childhood friend, and suddenly cannot pick up the scalpel.
anchor_text: Pause when grief overwhelms; stillness precedes clarity.
